title: 15-year-old Charlestown girl reported missing
author: John Waller
bullets: 15-year-old Hayden Fletcher was last seen Monday at 5:30 a.m. near 13th Street in Charlestown.
Hayden Fletcher is described as 5-foot-4, 128 pounds, with blue eyes and dark blond hair.
Anyone with information on Hayden Fletcher's whereabouts is urged to contact 911 or Downtown Detectives at 617-343-4571.

title: Stoneham police arrest Watertown man for alleged indecent exposure at Michaels craft store
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Andrey Pavlov, 44, of Watertown was arrested for allegedly exposing himself at businesses on Main Street in Stoneham.
One incident occurred at a Michaels craft store; police did not disclose the other business involved.
Pavlov pleaded not guilty to charges of open and gross lewdness in Woburn District Court.

title: Dalia brings wood-fired Spanish cuisine to South Boston
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Dalia, a new Spanish restaurant in South Boston, focuses on wood-fired cuisine using Josper grills sourced from Spain.
The menu at Dalia includes tapas, paella, and Spanish wines, with prices ranging from $9 to $170 for various dishes.
The restaurant offers a unique dining experience with multiple seating areas and plans to operate as a cafe during the day.

title: Randolph movie theater sells for $9 million
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Core Investments bought the shuttered Showcase Cinema de Lux in Randolph for $9 million from National Amusements.
The 112,265 sq ft theater property, located on Route 139, features 16 screens and was a top-performing site.
Future plans for the cinema site are undetermined as Core Investments negotiates with a potential tenant.

title: One of the top 3 road cycling events in the U.S. is in Vermont, according to USA Today readers
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The Farm to Fork Gran Fondo in Vermont ranked No. 3 among the top U.S. road cycling events.
The ride covers 60 miles in Lake Champlain, stopping at four farms for gourmet bites.
Proceeds from a pre-ride dinner support local farm and community programs.

title: Suspect arrested in stabbing near Northeastern University
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Stephen Dantzler, 58, arrested in connection with a stabbing near Northeastern University; charged with assault and battery with a dangerous weapon.
The stabbing occurred near Huntington Ave. and Gainsborough St.; the victim was hospitalized with nonlife-threatening injuries.
Incident took place off-campus; victim unaffiliated with Northeastern entered a university building for assistance after being stabbed.

title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum star in blowout Celtics win over Heat: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 43 points, overwhelming the Miami Heat in a 147-129 victory.
Jayson Tatum achieved a triple-double performance with 25 points, 18 rebounds, and 11 assists, contributing to the Celtics' win.
Neemias Queta scored 16 points and grabbed 11 rebounds, showing improved defensive discipline against Bam Adebayo.

title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox off to worst start since 2019 with a 1-5 record after sweep by the Astros.
Red Sox pitchers, including new signings, have allowed 11 earned runs and 11 homers in six games.
Boston's defense commits six errors in six games, continuing last season's league-high error trend.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
Diggs faces charges related to a December dispute with his personal chef over unpaid wages.
The Patriots have stated their support for Diggs, who has denied the allegations against him.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police Sgt. Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide after a 2023 crash in Woburn killed Angelo Schettino.
Middlesex DA's office was not notified of Quigley's alleged drunk driving despite reporting requirements for serious accidents.
An internal investigation revealed crucial information about the crash was missing from State Police reports and government documents.
